title: Interactive cold-start / bootstrap (existing repo → proposed starter wiki)
description:
Drop the skill into a repo with history → a PROPOSED starter wiki, with the user involved at two explicit gates before any mining. Propose-not-apply, stingy, always-MECE.

Flow: GATE 1 agree structure (MECE, adapted to this repo's signals, recorded in the resolver) → scaffold (kb init) → GATE 2 agree ingestion scope per source using REAL COUNTS (chats/commits/docs/code) → mine within scope → propose a small starter set (migrate docs, chat backfill via extraction Prompt 1, decision-shaped commits, code orientation + a few from-code caches; mark gaps, don't fabricate) → user reviews/approves → commit.

Build: read-only `kb bootstrap` report (structure signals + source counts), references/bootstrap.md interactive protocol (2 gates + mining prompt), SKILL.md wiring. Stdlib, propose-not-apply, MECE. Context: docs/ideas/repo-wiki.md, references/{structure,extraction,claude-md-shim}.md, scripts/kb.py (cmd_init, cmd_catchup, count_uningested_chat_sessions).
closed_reason: Completed: interactive cold-start bootstrap. kb bootstrap (read-only signals+counts, --json); references/bootstrap.md (GATE 1 MECE structure→resolver→init, GATE 2 per-source scope via counts, fanned-out parallel-subagent mining, propose-not-apply, gap-marked); SKILL/structure wiring. 3 ticks. Fixed a real defect: recall --limit (default 10) crowded out real sessions behind agent-* subagents → chat count undercounted (0→4); fixed across catchup/count/bootstrap.
